description_long: This is a military-style triplane with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring three stacked straight wings and a single propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is elongated and tapers toward the tail, which ends in a conventional tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two main wheels positioned beneath the lower wing and a small tail skid at the rear. The cockpit is located just behind the upper wing, likely open, and appears to be a single-seat configuration. The propeller is a two-blade design, and the overall construction suggests a lightweight, early 20th-century aircraft. The surface finish is matte, with visible angular geometry and a hard, metallic appearance.
description: A camouflage-painted triplane with three stacked wings, a single nose-mounted propeller, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is open and positioned behind the upper wing, with fixed landing gear and a matte, hard surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, three stacked wings, and the single propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the triple wing configuration, propeller, and the fixed landing gear beneath the lower wing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, emphasizing the tail assembly and the stacked wings.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tapering fuselage, conventional tail, and the three wings from behind.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, stacked wings; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward on the fuselage.
